rulings.Case Title: NORTHFIELD INSURANCE COMPANY VS. HONOR GUARD SECURITY SERVICES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mataese Togafau
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT EXPERT SERVICE PROFESSIONALS, INC.'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment; Defendant Expert Service Professionals, Inc.'s ("ESP") motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied. First, the motion is untimely. Code of Civil Procedure § 473.5 provides that the motion must be "filed within a reasonable time, but in no event exceeding the earlier of: (i) two years after entry of a default judgment against him or her; or (ii) 180 days after service on him or her of a written notice that the default or default judgment has been entered." In this case, the notice of entry of judgment was served on August 23, 2018, and plaintiff filed this untimely motion on June 19, 2019. Even if the court were to rely on the 2-year deadline, the court concludes that the motion is still untimely because ESP failed to move within "a reasonable time." The record demonstrates that ESP's Chief Financial Officer (Paul Alvarez) was served with an order of examination in January of 2019, but ESP failed to file this motion earlier. (Kirkgaard Decl. 28 and Ex. S.) Moreover, the record shows that Mr. Alvarez appeared in this action to contest service of an order of examination in March of 2018. (Id.) While it may be true that Mr. Alvarez was appearing in his individual capacity or on behalf of Telic Insurance Services at that time, constructive notice is still imputed to ESP as he was ESP's agent. Second, ESP fails to provide a proposed pleading in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure § 473.5(b). Third, even in light of the liberal policy to hear cases on the merits, ESP fails to carry its evidentiary burden of showing a lack of actual notice. The record discloses that numerous documents regarding this case had been properly served at ESP's office over the years. (Summons and complaint [8/29/16]; request for entry of default [11/4/16]; application for default judgment [10/24/17]; notice of entry of order granting default judgment [12/20/17].) The terse declaration of ESP's president (Mataese Togagau) does not sufficiently explain why or how these documents did not reach it. The court recognizes that mail may get lost or misplaced. ESP, however, concedes that the documents were mailed to the correct address, and the sheer number of documents that ESP allegedly did not receive makes its position implausible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: QUAN DO VS. THOMAS B GARBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Continued to July 31, 2019. Otis seeks to have the court confirm that the settlement is in good faith per Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, but has not provided a copy of the settlement to the court nor disclosed all of its material terms. Otis is ordered to file a motion to seal in compliance with CRC 2.550 et seq. by July 19, 2019 and personally or e-mail serve that motion by that date on all parties. The motion to seal shall include a copy of the settlement and explain why there is an overriding interest in sealing it or portions thereof. (See CRC 2.550(d).) The motion to seal will be heard on July 31, 2019. Opposition to that motion, if any, may be filed and personally or e-mail served by July 25, 2019. All parties to provide courtesy copies of their filings per SF Local Rule 2.7B. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JUNE E CLAUSEN VS. PACIFIC CRUISE SHIP TERMINALS LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Otis' Motion For Protective Order (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 6-17-19)
rulings.Rulings: July 15, 2019 Line 1 1) Otis' Motion For Protective Order (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 6-17-19) Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. In opposing the motion to compel heard by Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz, Otis sought to condition production of the manual on a protective order. Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz effectively denied this request in that he ordered production of the manual without requiring a protective order. Otis' motion does not meet the requirements for a motion for reconsideration under CCP 1008 in that it was filed more than 15 days after Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz's order was mailed and it presents no new facts, circumstances or law that could not have been presented in the opposition to the motion to compel heard by Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz. Despite the failure to comply with CCP 1008, the court has the inherent power to reconsider its ruling. Any reconsideration would have to be by Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz, not by another Judge. The Order requiring production of the manual is stayed through July 30, 2019. If by then, Otis fails to persuade either plaintiff to agree to a protective order for the manual or Judge Pro Tem Lebowitz to reconsider his order and condition production of the manual on a protective order, then the manual must be produced on July 31. This pro tem judge takes no position on whether Pro Tem Judge Lebowitz should reconsider his order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF HECTOR GANDHI'S Motion And Motion To Seal Grant. No opposition filed. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Arup North America Ltd. Motion For Summary Adjudication Against American Zurich Insurance Company And Zurich American Insurance Company (Tentative Rulings Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 6, Defendant Arup North America Ltd.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Against American Zurich Insurance Company And Zurich American Insurance Company (Tentative Rulings Only Part 2 Of 2) Zurich argues that despite these allegations, extrinsic evidence shows that Arup really only performed professional services. Zurich cites the "Architect-Subconsultant Agreement For Geotechnical Services," the Pelli Clark Pelli Architects, Inc. contract, and several "admissions" from Arup's general counsel (Joseph Dennis). This evidence does not conclusively eliminate the potential for coverage. Even if the contracts originally contemplated that Arup would only perform "professional services," it is possible that Arup subsequently performed other non-professional services too. Mr. Dennis' correspondence of November 18, 2016 did not establish that there was no potential for coverage. While he agreed that Zurich's recitation of the facts regarding the Millennium Tower claim was "generally correct," he further noted that some of Arup's work may not fall within the professional services exclusion. (Zurich's Index of Exhibits, Ex. 13.) Zurich has not met its heavy burden of showing that there was no potential for coverage. "To prevail, the insured must prove the existence of a potential for coverage, while the insurer must establish the absence of any such potential. In other words, the insured need only show that the underlying claim may fall within policy coverage; the insurer must prove it cannot. Facts merely tending to show that the claim is not covered, or may not be covered, but are insufficient to eliminate the possibility that resultant damages (or the nature of the action) will fall within the scope of coverage, therefore add no weight to the scales." (Montrose, 6 Cal.4th at 300; see also North Counties Engineering, Inc. v. State Farm General Ins. Co. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 902, 928 [allegations of negligent construction of dam were outside the scope of the professional services exclusion]; Atlantic Mutual Ins. Co. v. J. Lamb, Inc. (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1038-1039 ["An insurer may rely on an exclusion to deny coverage only if it provides conclusive evidence demonstrating that the exclusion applies." (emphasis in original)].) Because Zurich has failed to provide conclusive evidence demonstrating that the exclusion applies, Arup's motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling part 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Arup North America Ltd. Motion For Summary Adjudication Against American Zurich Insurance Company And Zurich American Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 6, Defendant Arup North America Ltd. Motion For Summary Adjudication Against American Zurich Insurance Company And Zurich American Insurance Company (PART 1 OF 2) Defendant Arup North America Ltd.'s ("Arup") motion for summary adjudication on its second cause of action for declaratory relief in its second amended cross-complaint against American Zurich Insurance Company and Zurich American Insurance Company ("Zurich") is granted. Zurich owes a duty to defend Arup in the underlying Millennium Tower property damage cases. Arup purchased general liability insurance policies from Zurich from April 2008 through April 2014. (UMF 1.) Those policies provide coverage when "'property damage' is caused by an 'occurrence' that takes place in the 'coverage territory.'" (UMF 3.) The policy also contains a professional services exclusion. (UMF 9.) Arup contends that it is entitled to coverage because the underlying actions allege that it negligently performed professional and non-professional services. Zurich argues that no coverage exists because extrinsic evidence shows that Arup is only being sued for damages based on its professional services. Arup demonstrates that it is entitled to coverage and Zurich owes a duty to defend as a matter of law. "The determination whether the insurer owes a duty to defend usually is made in the first instance by comparing the allegations of the complaint with the terms of the policy. Facts extrinsic to the complaint also give rise to a duty to defend when they reveal a possibility that the claim may be covered by the policy." (Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal.4th 287, 295.) "Any doubt as to whether the facts establish the existence of the defense duty must be resolved in the insured's favor." (Montrose, 6 Cal.4th at 299-300.) An insurer may rely on extrinsic evidence to show no coverage exists, but its adduced undisputed facts must "conclusively eliminate a potential for liability." (Id. at 299 (emphasis added).) In this case, the underlying allegations base liability on far more than professional services. The Millennium Tower Association alleges that: Arup breached its duty to exercise ordinary care. On information and belief, the HOA alleges that Arup negligently, carelessly, tortuously, and wrongfully failed to use reasonable care in the analysis, preparation, design, manufacture, and/or construction of the soils evaluation, lateral support, and/or buttress pile wall, thereby causing damage to the Millennium Tower… (Arup's Ex. M, 122 (emphasis added).) Treadwell & Rollo, Inc.'s cross-complaint for indemnity against Arup alleges that it "recklessly and negligently performed design and/or construction activities at the Transit Center site, including, but not limited to, dewatering, excavation, demolition, shoring, and other activities." (Arup's Ex. N, 23.) Mission Street Development LLC and Millennium Partners Management LLC similarly averred that Arup's construction activities caused damage. (Arup's Ex. P, 2, 141, 223, 259, and 262.) (end of tentative ruling part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN ANN MASON ET AL VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Leave To File Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFFS DAWN MASON, JAMES MASON'S Motion Requesting Leave To File Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Plaintiffs Dawn Mason and James Mason's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion conforms to California's strong policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings and Defendants have not shown that they would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. (See Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) Defendant's objections to the amendments can be addressed through pleading motions. Defendant has not shown that a trial continuance is warranted at this stage of the proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Chute'S First Set Of Requests For Admission, First Set Of Form Interrogatories, First Set Of Requests For Production Of Docs, And First Set Of Special Interrogatories To Pltf And X-Deft Kevin Dorgan
rulings.Rulings: July 15, 2019 Line 2 DEFENDANT EDMUND CHUTE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Chute'S First Set Of Requests For Admission, First Set Of Form Interrogatories, First Set Of Requests For Production Of Docs, And First Set Of Special Interrogatories To Pltf And X-Deft Kevin Dorgan Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: RFA 10-12 and 27: Granted. Objections overruled. Relevance is very broad in discovery. The accuracy of the assertions plaintiff made in the mechanic's liens is relevant, at least for purposes of discovery. RFA 28: Granted. Objection overruled. Dorgan admitted he didn't work at the property after May 24, 2018. But he did not respond to the part of the RFA asking him to admit that no one working at his direction worked at the property after May 24, 2018. The response to this part of the RFA should be "Admitted," "Denied," or "a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter in this request has been made, and the information known or readily obtainable is insufficient to enable me to admit the matter." RFA 29 and 30: Granted. Whether a permit was issued for the work is relevant, at least for purposes of discovery. /Form Interrogatory 17.1: Granted./Document Requests 23, 24 and 31: Granted./ Interrogatories 8 and 9: Granted./ Interrogatories 17-19: Denied. Attorney-client privilege. However, if plaintiff refuses to answer on this basis, he is barred from introducing any evidence in this case as why he and his attorney did not verify the mechanic's liens or as to why he caused mechanic's lien II to be filed. The court reserves jurisdiction over this matter to see that the Order is complied with. The ruling on sanctions is reserved until the Order is complied with. All responses and documents required by this Order shall be verified and served by August 5, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: J GARCIA CARRION, SA ET AL VS. VINCENT FRIEND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order For Vincent Friend And Kathleen Friend To Show Cause Why They Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of The Judgment Entered July 10, 2018
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFFS J GARCIA CARRION, SA, CIV USA, INC'S Motion For An Order For Vincent Friend And Kathleen Friend To Show Cause Why They Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of The Judgment Entered July 10, 2018 Plaintiffs J. Garcia Carrion, SA (JGC) and CIV USA's motion for an order for Defendants Vincent Friend and Kathleen Friend to show cause why they should not be held in contempt of the judgment entered July 10, 2018 is denied. A determination that a person is guilty of contempt under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1209(a)(5) and 1218 requires a showing "beyond a reasonable doubt." (Mitchell v. Superior Court (1989) 49 Cal.3d 1230, 1256.) A judgment of contempt must meet strict requirements: (1) knowledge of the judgment, (2) ability to comply with it, and (3) willful disobedience. (In re Jones (1975) 47 Cal.App.3d 879, 881.) The Court cannot conclude beyond a reasonable doubt that Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend willfully disobeyed the July 10, 2018 judgment confirming the Arbitration Award. As an initial matter, Ms. Friend was not even a party to the Award and judgment upon which the contempt request is based, so it is not proper to hold her in contempt for violating that judgment. (Rivas Decl., Ex. 2.) Furthermore, although Defendants previously acknowledged reading the Award as requiring transfer of the civusa.com domain name, these representations do not create a sufficient basis to hold Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend in contempt. The interpretation of the Award became subject to a legal dispute that was not resolved until the Court's April 23, 2019 order regarding the demurrer to JGC's first amended complaint. (Rivas Decl., Ex. 6.) That order concluded that "full rights" to the CIV USA name, as stated in the Award, included the right to the domain name and referenced statements by Defendants' counsel as evidence for reading the Award in this way. (Id.) However, this resolution of the legal dispute regarding the obligations imposed by the Award, even in light of counsel's apparent acquiescence to JGC's reading of the Award, cannot apply retroactively to establish beyond a reasonable doubt that Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend willfully disobeyed the July 10, 2018 judgment. This is especially true given that the judgment merely stated that the Award was confirmed; neither the judgment nor the underlying Award provided an explicit, narrowly-tailored Court directive to transfer the domain name. (See In re Marcus (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1009, 1014 ["Any ambiguity in a decree or order must be resolved in favor of an alleged contemnor."]; Wilson v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 1259, 1273 ["[P]unishment [for contempt] can only rest upon clear, intentional violation of a specific narrowly drawn order. Specificity is an essential prerequisite of a contempt citation."]; Butler v. Superior Court (1960) 178 Cal.App.2d 763, 765 [a party cannot be held guilty for violating an order that is "uncertain or ambiguous"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE VS. CREATIVE DIVIRSION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-18-569890 WITH CASE CGC-19-574397
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-18-569890 WITH CASE CGC-19-574397 Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ORCA USA, LTD, AN INDIANA CORPORATION VS. RICHARD MOORE ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT RICHARD MOORE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Off calendar. First Amended Complaint filed July 2, 2019. Plaintiff's counsel is admonished that per Code of Civil Procedure Section 472(a), the amended complaint was due to be filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer, which was July 1. =(302/EPS)
